metadata.title: UUID Generator — Generate UUID v4, v1, Bulk UUIDs Online
metadata.description: Free online UUID generator. Create UUID v4 (random), v1 (timestamp), bulk generate up to 100, and validate UUIDs. 100% browser-based.
metadata.keywords: uuid generator, uuid v4, uuid v1, generate uuid online, random uuid, bulk uuid, guid generator, unique id generator, uuid validator

heading: UUID Generator
subheading: Generate random UUIDs (v4), timestamp-based UUIDs (v1), or bulk generate up to 100. All generated in your browser.

seo.section1Text: A UUID (Universally Unique Identifier) is a 128-bit number used to uniquely identify information in computer systems. Also known as GUID (Globally Unique Identifier), UUIDs are formatted as 32 hexadecimal digits in five groups separated by hyphens: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx. The probability of generating duplicate UUIDs is so low that they can be considered practically unique.
seo.section2Text: UUID v1 is generated from the current timestamp and a node identifier (typically a MAC address). UUID v4 is generated using random or pseudo-random numbers and is the most commonly used version. UUID v4 has 122 random bits, giving approximately 5.3 × 10^36 possible values — making collisions virtually impossible.
seo.section3Text: UUIDs are used as database primary keys (especially in distributed systems), API resource identifiers, session tokens, file names, message queue IDs, and anywhere you need a unique identifier without coordination between systems. UUID v4 is recommended for most use cases due to its simplicity and randomness.
seo.section4List: Database primary keys (PostgreSQL, MongoDB) | API resource identifiers | Distributed system node IDs | Session and correlation IDs | File and object naming | Message queue deduplication

ui.howItWorksText: Click Generate to create a new UUID. Choose v4 (random) or v1 (timestamp-based). Use Bulk to generate multiple UUIDs at once. Paste a UUID in the validator to check its format and version.
ui.v4Desc: Cryptographically random — recommended for most uses
ui.v1Desc: Based on current time — sortable by creation time
ui.validateTitle: Check if a string is a valid UUID
